name: Heineken
cultural_notes: A standard 'green bottle' lager recognized globally. Known for consistent quality control across borders.
key_safety_notes: Available in non-alcoholic (Heineken 0.0) in many regions

name: Bud Light
cultural_notes: One of the best-selling light beers globally. Uses rice as an adjunct to keep the body light and refreshing.
key_safety_notes: Fast consumption due to light body
High water content contributes to slower intoxication vs heavy ales
recommended_actions: Monitor pace of consumption due to drinkability

name: Budweiser
cultural_notes: Known as the 'King of Beers,' it uses rice as an adjunct for a cleaner, crisper finish that appeals to a massive global audience.

name: Corona Extra
cultural_notes: Traditionally served with a lime wedge. The clear bottle is iconic but makes the beer susceptible to 'skunking' if left in direct sunlight.
key_safety_notes: Slightly lower ABV than standard European lagers
Transparent bottle leads to rapid flavor degradation in sun
recommended_actions: Keep out of direct sunlight to maintain flavor

name: Miller Lite
cultural_notes: Marketed as the original light beer (1975). It maintains a slightly more floral pilsner profile compared to other light lagers.
key_safety_notes: High drinkability may lead to a faster pace of consumption
recommended_actions: Monitor consumption pace due to light body

name: Stella Artois
cultural_notes: Originally brewed as a special Christmas beer in Leuven, named 'Stella' (Latin for Star) after the Christmas Star.
key_safety_notes: Higher carbonation level than some domestic lagers
